items.title: Turn on Turborepo remote caching in CI
items.why: Remote caching shares task outputs across teammates and CI runners, so unchanged builds/tests/lints download instead of re-running, collapsing duplicated work on every PR.
how_to_steps: Define cacheable tasks in turbo.json with precise `inputs` and `outputs` so content hashes are correct.
Connect a remote cache: managed via `npx turbo login` + `npx turbo link`, or a self-hosted cache endpoint with a token.
Run a build twice locally and confirm the second run reports a remote cache hit.
In CI, pass the cache token/team env vars and run `turbo run build test lint`; verify the logs show 'remote cache hit'.
items.evidence: Makeswift cut CI pipeline time 65% (commit-to-deploy ~20 min -> under 1 min); the Next.js team cut publish times 80%; a 6-month monorepo retrospective reported full builds down ~50% (22 -> 11 min p50) at a 94% cache hit rate.
items.try_today: Enable remote caching on one repo and re-run a green build to confirm a remote cache hit.
